fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 60 is inverted: the clamping block executes when `clamp` is False (i.e., `not clamp` is True) instead of when `clamp` is True, so clamping is applied when it should not be and skipped when it should be.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the branch body (clamping) should execute, so `not clamp` should be False when clamp is True — this is violated by the inverted condition.
The clamping branch should only be entered when clamp is False (no-clamp / extrapolation mode) — but the logic is inverted so this branch is entered when clamp=True.
y__2 is the clamped value; it should only be computed when clamp is False (extrapolation disabled), not when clamp is True.

spec.expr: (clamp == True) or (y__3 >= min(y0, y1) and y__3 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, y__3 should be the unclamped interpolation (possibly outside [y0,y1]); when clamp=False it should be clamped within [y0,y1] — the inverted condition causes the wrong assignment.
